name: Safe Room
description: Safe Room is a fear-building mechanic that shapes vulnerability, uncertainty, attention, and tension.
design_notes: Safe Room should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For horror mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use safe room as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect safe room to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels vulnerable, alert, and uncertain about what happens next.

parameters.name: tension_level
parameters.description: Current pressure or fear intensity used to drive the mechanic.

parameters.name: resource_drain
parameters.description: Rate at which a safety or visibility resource is consumed.

parameters.name: reveal_distance
parameters.description: Distance at which a threat or cue becomes clear.

edge_cases: Safe Room is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Safe Room overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Safe Room runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Safe Room state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Safe Room plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Safe Room produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Safe Room strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Safe Room becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Safe Room with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Safe Room as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Safe Room with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Safe Room as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
